items.description: Navigating to the record stock page renders a form with input fields for SKU, location, quantity, and tracking code
Submitting the form with a required field left blank shows an inline validation error that names the missing field
Submitting the form with all valid inputs creates a stock record in the database with the submitted SKU, location, quantity, and tracking code
After a successful form submission the user sees a confirmation message indicating the stock record was saved
A tracking code submitted on the form is retrievable from the API without loss or truncation
Submitting the form for a SKU and location that already has a record updates the existing record's quantity and shows no error message
Submitting a stock record with a negative quantity is rejected at write time and no row with a negative quantity is stored
The routes/boundary module does not import the DB session; persistence is only reachable through the repository layer (layering contract)
The application reads DB_NAME/PGDATABASE from the environment and contains no hardcoded DSN string (config-in-env, NFR-F1-5)
The stock table carries a database-level unique constraint on (sku, location) so the upsert strategy is enforced at the schema layer (NFR-F1-3)
The pytest-bdd conftest binds to the real paired-branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory DB substitution is used in any integration test (NFR-F1-4)
Navigating to the stock home page when records exist renders a table with one row per seeded stock record
Navigating to the stock home page when no records exist shows an explicit empty-state guidance message instead of a blank or empty table
When records span multiple locations the table rows are grouped so all rows for one location appear together before rows for the next location
Each table row displays the SKU, location, quantity, and inventory_code values from the corresponding seeded stock record
A stock record whose inventory_code is null renders the literal text 'not tracked' in the inventory_code column rather than an empty cell (NFR-F1-6)
The rendered quantity cell carries a right-align CSS style so the numeric column is visually scannable
The home route module does not import the DB session; the read path reaches the database only through the repository layer (layering contract)
The repository or service module supplies rows already ordered by location then SKU; no sorting or grouping logic appears in the boundary or template module
Navigating to the detail page for a SKU that has stock at multiple locations renders one row per location with the location name and current quantity
Each location row in the SKU detail view displays the inventory tracking code stored on that location's stock record
A location row whose optional fields (e.g., par level) have no stored value displays the literal text 'not tracked' rather than a blank cell or raw null
The SKU detail route module does not import the DB session; the read path is enforced as boundary -> service -> repository with no ORM access outside the repository (layering contract, NFR-F1-7)

Seeding stock for a SKU at N locations and requesting the detail view returns exactly N rows with matching location, quantity, and tracking code for each seeded record, with no records omitted (read completeness, NFR-F1-7)
